If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

Level 2: you have used 20 of about 48 target actions so far.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 10 actions returned the board to a state already seen 10 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 10 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Game over again! The problem is that collecting all green blocks in one batch causes game over. The batch executed 8 actions but only some were processed before game over.
Looking at the executed actions: 8 MOUSE clicks were executed. But the game went to game_over state.
The issue seems to be that the purple bar (timer) fills up too quickly when I batch too many actions. Each action increments the purple bar, and when it reaches 64, game over.
I need to collect blocks one at a time and check for the pink cross after each collection. Or I need to understand what triggers the pink cross to appear.
In Level 1, the pink cross appeared after collecting all green blocks at a specific player position. In Level 2, maybe the pink cross appears after collecting ALL green blocks across ALL positions.
But the game keeps going to game_over before I can collect all blocks. This suggests:
1. The purple bar starts at a higher value in Level 2, or
2. Each action adds more to the purple bar in Level 2, or
3. I need a different strategy
Let me try collecting blocks one at a time and checking for the pink cross after each one. This will be slower but might prevent game over.
Actually, wait. Looking at the executed actions more carefully:
- First batch: 9 actions executed (MOUSE clicks), game over
- Then I clicked 1 more block (greens: 14 -> 13)
- Second batch: 8 actions executed, game over again
But the green blocks are still visible in the current image (14 blocks shown). This means the blocks are respawning somehow!
Let me check the current state more carefully. The image shows all green blocks present. But I collected some of them...
Oh wait, I think I understand now. The "game over" state resets the board! When the game goes to game_over, all collected blocks respawn. That's why the green blocks are still visible.
So the strategy should be:
1. Collect blocks one at a time
2. Check for pink cross after each collection
3. If pink cross appears, navigate to it immediately
4. Don't let the purple bar fill up
Let me try collecting just 1 block at a time.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: Game over again - blocks respawn when game over. Need to collect blocks one at a time to avoid purple bar filling up.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Collect blocks one at a time, checking for pink cross after each
seg = current_frame.segmentation
greens = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
pink = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'P']
purple_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p']
purple_len = purple_nodes[0]['pixels'] if purple_nodes else 0
print(f"Start: greens={len(greens)}, pink={len(pink)}, purple={purple_len}")

collected = 0
for i in range(5):  # Try up to 5 blocks
    if not greens:
        break
    g = greens[0]
    boundary = g['boundary']
    center_row = sum(p[0] for p in boundary) // len(boundary)
    center_col = sum(p[1] for p in boundary) // len(boundary)
    
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_row, 'col': center_col}])
    collected += 1
    
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    greens = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
    pink = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'P']
    purple_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p']
    purple_len = purple_nodes[0]['pixels'] if purple_nodes else 0
    
    print(f"After click {collected}: greens={len(greens)}, pink={len(pink)}, purple={purple_len}, game_over={result.get('game_over')}")
    
    if result.get('game_over'):
        print("GAME OVER - stopping")
        break
    
    if pink:
        print("PINK CROSS FOUND!")
        p = pink[0]
        p_boundary = p['boundary']
        pink_col = sum(pt[1] for pt in p_boundary) // len(p_boundary)
        player = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b'][0]
        player_boundary = player['boundary']
        player_col = sum(pt[1] for pt in player_boundary) // len(player_boundary)
        print(f"Pink at col {pink_col}, player at col {player_col}")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
